He has attacked Zelensky, Macron, Meloni, Trudeau, Starmer, and four Republican senators. The record speaks for itself.

Does the behavioral record of a president who has never once criticized Vladimir Putin while attacking every American ally constitute functional asset behavior regardless of whether there was formal recruitment?

"Asset" in intelligence terminology means someone whose actions serve a foreign power's interests, whether wittingly or not. By that definition the behavioral record is fully documented. By the legal definition of a recruited spy, no public evidence establishes it.

record.text:
a great dissection of why a lot of fandom drama doesn't only boil down to "these people are thinking too much about [insert series here] and need to touch grass." 

there's another layer to the way folks get obsessed (for parasocial/self-important rewriting/clout reasons, etc) that gets glossed over

images.alt:
by asneakyfox on tumblr:

one big thing i think people outside fandom (like, all fandoms, fandom in general, not any particular one) tend to misunderstand is they know it's a subculture of people who are weirdly deeply invested in fictional media, and they hear about drama caused by people in those subcultures being unhinged in not-fun ways, and they think the unhingedness comes from the fact of being overinvested in works of fiction.

which is a natural assumption, but in my experience that's not really the case? like in my experience the drama llamas in fandom are usually not the ones who are just genuinely very deeply into the fiction. i've known people who are basically thinking about star trek or x-men comics or supernatural pretty much 100% of their free time and ime that type of person is usually very nice and surprisingly functional in their regular life. when someone's a constant nexus of fandom drama it's usually not that they are obsessed with the actual work of fiction the fandom is about, it's at least one of the following:

what they're obsessed with is not the source material but their unhealthy parasocial relationships with one or more of the people who created it

what they're obsessed with is not the source material but some elaborate shared-universe subset of fanfic about it that's only barely related to the original at this point, and/or an esoteric reading-against-the-text reinterpretation of the source material (often if the canon is active and ongoing this leads to becoming actively hostile toward it for its inevitably increasing failure to conform to their preferred fanon)

what they're obsessed with is not the source material but the fandom itself and gathering clout within it, so that the source material basically only exists to them as a tool for scoring points in increasingly arcane fandom disputes

and very often you get the same person doing 2 and sometimes even all 3 of these, and that's where the trouble really starts.

external.description:
このX投稿の補足と解説です。

投稿の内容まとめ
投稿者: @Wladislao_Ghcb（地理・歴史・政治などに詳しいアカウント）
引用元: @M_Nishiyokohamaの投稿（ソマリランド大統領のイスラエル訪問に関するSmartNews記事）
主旨: ソマリランドは事実上独立した未承認国家で、国際的にほとんど認められていないが、イスラエルが最近承認したという点の解説。

ソマリランドの背景（補足）
ソマリランド（Somaliland）は、アフリカの角（Horn of Africa）に位置する地域で、1991年にソマリアから事実上独立を宣言しました。

地理: 旧イギリス領ソマリランドの領域。現在のソマリア連邦共和国の北西部（アデン湾に面する）。
人口: 約400-600万人程度（推定）。
首都: ハルゲイサ（Hargeisa）。
特徴:
  比較的安定した統治を行い、独自の通貨（ソマリランド・シリング）、議会、軍、選挙を実施。
  ソマリア本土とは異なり、イスラム過激派の影響が少なく、比較的穏健。
  民主主義的な体制を維持しようとする努力が評価される一方、国際法上はソマリアの一部と見なされ、国家承認はほぼゼロ。

友好関係:
エチオピア、ケニア、ジブチ（周辺国）とは実務的な協力関係。
台湾とは正式な外交関係を持ち、相互に代表機関を置いている（中国の圧力で多くの国が台湾と断交する中、珍しい例）。

イスラエルとの関係の意義
投稿の核心である「イスラエルが承認した」点について：

イスラエルは2020年代に入り、アフリカの未承認国家・準国家との関係強化を進めています。ソマリランド大統領の訪イスラエルは、戦略的・地政学的な意味が大きいです。

主なポイント:
紅海・アデン湾の安全保障: ソマリランドは紅海の出口（バブ・エル・マンデブ海峡）に近く、近年フーシ派（イエメン）による船舶攻撃が問題化。イスラエルにとって重要な海上交通路の安定化に寄与する可能性。
反イスラム過激派の協力: ソマリランドはアルシャバーブなどのテロ組織に抵抗しており、イスラエル（および西側諸国）と利害が一致。
外交的突破: イスラエルが未承認国家を正式に（または準公式に）認める動きは、アラブ諸国との関係正常化（アブラハム合意）の延長線上でも注目される。
台湾との類似: 両者とも「中国・ソマリアの領土主張に抵抗する存在」として、イスラエルが戦略的に接近しやすい。

現在の状況（2026年6月時点）
ソマリランドは依然として完全な国家承認を得ていないが、イスラエルをはじめ一部の国・地域との実務的・軍事・経済的つながりが深まっている。
アフリカの角地域は、地政学的緊張（エチオピアの港湾アクセス問題、紅海危機など）が続いており、ソマリランドの地位は今後も注目されるでしょう。

この投稿は、ニュース記事の補足として「未承認国家の実態」と「イスラエルの外交戦略」を簡潔に説明した内容と言えます。地政学に興味がある人向けの解説ポストですね。

external.description: Researchers propose turning a future NASA moon base into a quarantine station that would screen space samples before they ever reach Earth. A policy paper argues that NASA’s planned moon base should include a biocontainment facility to help protect Earth from potentially hazardous biological contaminants brought back from space. “Humanity is entering a new era of space exploration, but our planetary protection strategies have not kept pace with the risks associated with returning extraterrestrial samples to Earth,” said paper coauthor Frederick I. Moxley, Director of Strategic Threat Analysis and Research Laboratories, an Idaho-based consultancy. “The proposed facility would essentially act as a firewall between Earth and any potentially hazardous live organisms that could accompany returning future space missions,” said Moxley, whose coauthor is Anthony Ricciardi, a James McGill Professor of Biology and the Director of the Bieler School of Environment at McGill University. Samples would stop on the moon In their paper, published in Ambio, Moxley and Ricciardi argue that material gathered from the moon, Mars or farther destinations should not be sent straight to Earth. external.description: Coastal Georgia and South Carolina offer a variety of beaches on Tybee, Hilton Head, Jekyll, and St. Simons islands. Each island and its individual beaches have different personalities, from family-friendly to quiet and secluded. Tybee Island's five beaches range from the quiet, nature-focused Savannah River Beach to the popular, amenity-rich South Beach. Protecting elephants helps safeguard India's forests as powerful carbon stores: Study
->The Hindu | More on "Elephants protecting India's forest carbon" at BigEarthData.ai | #Elephant #Wildlife #Forest

external.description: Protecting the endangered Asiatic elephant (Elephas maximus indicus) indirectly helps safeguard India’s forests as powerful carbon stores, a new scientific study has said. The research, however, warned that long-term carbon stabilisation cannot be ensured merely by declaring more areas as elephant reserves but by improving habitat quality, restoring wildlife corridors, and strengthening forest management. Carbon stabilisation, a vital mechanism for removing greenhouse gases and mitigating climate change, is the process of trapping atmospheric carbon and locking it into stable, solid forms (such as in soil or organic matter). This helps resist decay, erosion, or the release of carbon back into the atmosphere. The study, published in the latest issue of the Journal of Threatened Taxa, assessed the relationship between elephant conservation efforts, the expansion of elephant reserves, and wildlife-associated carbon stock enhancement in India from 1992 to 2025. Noida-based Amity University’s Tarun Kathula and Tanu Jindal are its authors. The researchers found that India’s elephant reserve network expanded from 18,297 sq. km. (across three elephant reserves) to 80,777 sq. km. (33 elephant reserves), while the elephant population increased by about 6.7% from 25,604 estimated during 1992-93. Despite the marginal increase in the number of pachyderms, the estimated carbon stored within...
external.title: Protecting elephants helps safeguard India's forests as powerful carbon stores: Study

images.alt:
Bilhete em flip chart deixado pela seleção do Irã no vestiário do estádio de Seattle durante a Copa do Mundo 2026.

Escrito à mão em inglês, com marcador azul e anotações em vermelho, o texto é uma declaração de valores esportivos e culturais. A seleção afirma vir de uma terra que, há milênios, coloca a honra acima da vitória. Para eles, o futebol é um teste de caráter, não apenas de resultados. Pontos podem ser conquistados de muitas formas — respeito, não. O fair play, dizem, não é apenas uma regra: é a alma do jogo.

Ao final, agradecem a Seattle pela hospitalidade e aos torcedores iranianos que deram seus corações e vozes pelo país. A mensagem se encerra com a frase: *"Iran, always standing tall."*

Em vermelho, hashtags referenciam países adversários ou parceiros — #Austria, #Algeria, #Ghana, #Congo, #Uzbekistan, #Croatia — além de #FairPlay e #hero. O tom é sereno, altivo e profundamente humano.

record.text:
"Helpful content" is easy to hide behind.

Helpful for whom?

At what stage?

For what decision?

A founder learning AEO needs one page.

A growth lead comparing platforms needs another.

Give both the same guide and you've created fog.

People talk about optimizing for AI like each tool exists alone.

They don't.

ChatGPT needs sources.

Perplexity needs citations.

Gemini needs confidence.

That comes from reviews, communities, third-party mentions.

People talking about you when you're not in the room.

AI systems don't just need information.

They need decision-stage evidence.

Who is this product for?

What does it compare to?

What do customers say?

A generic blog post explains.

A strong BOFU page helps someone choose.

Publishers wouldn't commission a 'cosy murder' book deal,  even if a writer a) had a creative writing MFA specialising in cosy murder fiction b) had committed cosy, real-life murders then c) reformed, became a detective, and solved 100s of murders... unless they also had a daytime TV gardening show.
Ignore me, I'm just being bitter and twisted because I haven't found a home for my last novel yet, and the one I'm working on is a Sisyphean slog. And I'm barefoot. And the mountain is all Lego.

MFA in creative writing ❌️
Years dedicated to reading literature ❌️
